text: Dot and I got rained out halfway through our walk this morning, so add dried elderflower and oat straw to that list, would you hon? For the tea blends.
Will do! Also Emily said her clinical rotation wraps Friday, she's nervous about her final eval.
She'll be fine, tell her her grandmother started out just as green and lived to tell the tale. Nerves just mean she cares.
Ha, I'll pass that along. Can Tyler ride with you to practice Thursday, my car's making that noise again?
Course he can. Bring the car by David's Saturday, he'll take a look before I fuss at you about the mechanic.
Deal. Enjoy your Call the Midwife tonight, don't stay up past your bedtime for once 😄
Hey Mom, Emily said her first clinical shift at Providence went almost 12 hours straight. She sounded wiped when she called me.
She did good, Brian. I told her rounds felt exactly like that back when I was starting out at Emanuel, ha. Feet aching but proud as anything.
Glad she's got you to call after, not just me nodding along not knowing what she's talking about lol
That's what grandmothers are for. Dot Henderson stopped by our walk this morning just to hear about it too, she's still got opinions from her nursing days
Ha, tell Dot I said hi. Hailey wants to know if you're still doing the salve thing for her scout badge project
Tell that girl yes, calendula's already drying on the porch. Now hush, my program's starting, Mrs. Fletcher's about to solve another one 📺
Joanne, Emily just texted me from the hospital - survived her first clinical rotation shift lol
Oh that's my girl!! Tell her the first shift is always the worst, it gets easier 💕
She said her feet are killing her, any of your old nurse tricks?
Epsom soak and elevate 'em, works better than anything from a pharmacy. Tell her Grandma said so
Will do. Also Karen wants to know if you're still walking with Dot every morning
Like clockwork, rain or shine, 7am sharp - that woman keeps me honest
